description: An iOS and Android app providing high-quality text-to-speech, word prediction, and formatting controls to assist readers and writers with dyslexia.
title: ClaroSpeak

description: A multi-platform print reader for iOS and Android using advanced OCR to instantly convert printed text to speech or braille, supporting users with dyslexia and visual impairments.
title: KNFB Reader

description: An Android app that provides text-to-speech support for PDF documents while preserving original formatting and images, ideal for dyslexic users reading PDFs.
title: vBookz PDF Voice Reader

description: A companion app for iOS and Android offering reading and writing assistance, including text-to-speech and word prediction, for individuals with dyslexia.
title: TextHelp Read & Write

description: A comprehensive literacy support tool offering text-to-speech, vocabulary assistance, and proofing features to help students with dyslexia read, write, and understand text more effectively.
title: Read&Write by Texthelp

description: A digital literacy tool providing text-to-speech for web reading and personalized text appearance controls to support dyslexic readers online.
title: ReadSpeaker TextAid

description: A dyslexia-friendly text customization tool offering color overlays, font adjustments, and speech outputs to enhance reading and conversation support.
title: ClaroRead

description: A health resource explaining how mobile phone reminders and low-tech lists can help adults with dyslexia who struggle with forgetfulness and time management, offering practical tech strategies.
title: NYU Health Guide: Dyslexia Tools and Support Strategies

description: A nonprofit education site providing a curated list of assistive technology for dyslexia, including specific reminders tools and apps to help with organization and daily task management.
title: Undersood - Dyslexia Support Hub with Reminders Tool Recommendations

description: Official product page for ClaroSpeak, a text-to-speech and word prediction app available on iOS and Android that offers customizable fonts and audio recording to help dyslexic students practice reading and writing.
title: ClaroSpeak

description: Official product page for Read2Go, the mobile app for Bookshare users on iOS (and Android via alternate) that offers accessible, text-to-speech-enabled books for individuals with reading disabilities like dyslexia.
title: Read2Go

description: Documentation and strategy guide for using visual task initiation menus (IXC style) to break down reading and homework tasks into micro-steps for neurodivergent learners.
title: IXC Task Initiation Menu - Visual Chart

description: A support group app for parents of children with ASD, offering emotional support, practical tips for sensory regulation, and therapy guidance.
title: MyAutismTeam

description: A free accessible reading app for readers with dyslexia, low vision, or blindness, providing text-to-speech and adjustable reading parameters to support learning.
title: EasyReader

description: A freemium literacy app for ages 3-7 that introduces sight words and supports reading development through interactive games and audio feedback.
title: Endless Reader

description: A free app designed to help children with autism and sensory processing disorders communicate non-verbally, supporting language development and sensory regulation.
title: Sounding Board Language

description: A free sensory stimulation app for children with sensory processing disorders, using light, sound, and texture to help calm and regulate the body.
title: Dropophone

description: Purpose-built literacy support software for students, featuring text-to-speech, word prediction, dictation, and study tools to help read, write, and comprehend digital content across Windows.
title: Read&Write for Windows

description: Mac version of the leading literacy support software, enabling students to read locked files, images, PDFs, and web content with integrated text-to-speech, vocabulary support, and note-taking features.
title: Read&Write for Mac

description: Comprehensive reading, writing, and studying software built on UDL principles, offering text-to-speech, highlighting, annotation, and vocabulary support for students with diverse learning needs.
title: Kurzweil 3000

description: Computer-based oral reading fluency intervention that guides students through repeated readings of rich non-fiction content to build vocabulary, comprehension, and reading speed.
title: Read Naturally Live

description: Human-read audiobook library serving as a critical accommodation for students with text-based disabilities like dyslexia, while also supporting vocabulary growth and background knowledge development.
title: Learning Ally Audiobooks
